headline: Lead Contamination Found in 12% of Children's Toys Tested by MOH
context: MOH product safety testing found unsafe lead levels in 12% of children's toys sampled from Malaysian retailers.

cards.big: 12% of children's toys tested in 2025 exceeded WHO safe lead limits of 90 parts per million
cards.sub: Imported toys from unregulated sources pose highest contamination risk

cards.big: MOH tests only 2,400 toy products annually out of estimated 85,000 product lines in market
cards.sub: Testing covers less than 3% of available products leaving vast majority unchecked

cards.big: Lead exposure in children causes irreversible IQ reduction of 3-5 points per microgram
cards.sub: No safe level of lead exposure exists for developing brains

cards.big: EU RAPEX system recalls unsafe products within 48 hours across all 27 member states
cards.sub: Rapid alert network prevents contaminated products from remaining on shelves

cards.big: Children cannot read safety labels—product safety regulation is the only protection they have
Should Malaysia mandate pre-market safety testing for all children's products before retail sale
